AAPL.name: Apple Inc.
AAPL.description: Apple Inc. is an American multinational technology company that designs, manufactures, and markets consumer electronics, software, and online services. It is known for its innovative products such as the iPhone, iPad, Mac computers, Apple Watch, and Apple TV. The company also offers a range of services including the App Store, Apple Music, iCloud, and Apple Pay.
AAPL.history: In 1976, two young visionaries named Steve Jobs and Steve Wozniak started a small company in a California garage. They called it Apple Computer, and their dream was to make technology accessible to regular people. Their first major success came with the Apple II, which became one of the world's first highly successful mass-produced personal computers. As the years went by, Apple continued to push the boundaries of design and innovation. In 1984, they introduced the Macintosh, featuring a revolutionary graphical user interface that changed how people interacted with screens. Even through difficult financial periods in the 1990s, the company never stopped dreaming big. Steve Jobs returned to lead Apple into the new millennium, sparking a spectacular digital renaissance. The launch of the iPod in 2001 completely revolutionized the global music industry. Then, in 2007, Apple changed the world forever by introducing the iPhone, redefining modern communication and technology. Today, Apple is not just a computer manufacturer, but one of the most valuable tech giants on the planet. Its journey from a humble garage to a global powerhouse remains a timeless symbol of innovation and creative thinking.

MSFT.name: Microsoft Corporation
MSFT.description: Microsoft Corporation is an American multinational technology company that develops, manufactures, licenses, supports, and sells computer software, consumer electronics, and personal computers. It is one of the largest software companies in the world and offers a wide range of products and services, including Windows, Office, Azure, and Xbox. The company was founded by Bill Gates and Paul Allen in 1975 and has since become a global leader in various industries.
MSFT.history: In 1975, childhood friends Bill Gates and Paul Allen founded a small company called Microsoft with a bold vision: a computer on every desk and in every home. Their first massive breakthrough came in 1980, when they secured a contract with IBM to provide the operating system for their new personal computers. This system evolved into MS-DOS, establishing Microsoft as a dominant player in the tech industry. In 1985, the company launched Windows, introducing a user-friendly interface that quickly became the standard operating system worldwide. Throughout the 1990s, they expanded their empire with the Microsoft Office suite, redefining workplace productivity. Over the years, Microsoft also successfully entered the gaming world with the launch of the Xbox console in 2001. Under the modern leadership of Satya Nadella, the company shifted its focus heavily toward cloud computing with Microsoft Azure. Today, Microsoft is one of the most powerful tech giants, driving massive innovations in artificial intelligence. Its incredible journey from a two-man startup to a global leader shows the power of a clear vision.

GOOGL.name: Alphabet Inc.
GOOGL.description: Alphabet Inc. is an American multinational conglomerate that serves as the parent company of Google and several other subsidiaries. It was created in 2015 to restructure Google's various businesses and ventures. Alphabet's subsidiaries include Google, YouTube, Waymo (self-driving cars), Verily (life sciences), and many others. The company focuses on technology, innovation, and research in various fields.
GOOGL.history: In 1998, Larry Page and Sergey Brin, two Stanford University students, created a search engine called Google in a friend's garage. Their primary mission was simple yet ambitious: to organize the world's information and make it universally accessible and useful. Google's clean design and highly accurate search algorithms quickly made it the most popular search engine on the internet. As the company grew, it began expanding its ecosystem by launching game-changing services like Gmail and Google Maps. In 2008, they introduced the Android operating system, which eventually became the dominant software for smartphones globally. To better manage its massively diverse ventures, the company restructured in 2015, creating a parent company called Alphabet Inc. Today, Alphabet oversees not only Google and YouTube, but also highly futuristic projects like Waymo self-driving cars. The company is now a major global powerhouse heavily invested in machine learning and quantum computing. From a clever university research project, it has completely transformed how humanity accesses knowledge and interacts with the digital world.
